page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. The system provides 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core performance is average. However, the instance excels in specific compute and memory tasks. Passmark CPU encryption tests place it in the top 10%, and Passmark CPU score, extended instructions, and physics tests all rank in the top 25%. Memory performance is also notable, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth scoring in the top 25%. Software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks also demonstrate strong performance in the top 25%.

Given its high core count and integrated 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, this instance is highly suited for data-intensive workloads, database operations, and multi-threaded processing. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the top 25%, indicating strong suitability for high-throughput database hosting. The lack of AVX-512 support limits certain vector-heavy workloads, but the inclusion of local NVMe storage provides a cost-efficient alternative to network-attached storage for temporary data, scratch space, and caching.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While single-core benchmarks are modest, the instance delivers strong multi-core and cryptographic performance, with Passmark encryption tests ranking in the top 10%. It also achieves strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. With a 20 Gbps baseline network speed and high-speed local storage, this instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, and data processing workloads that benefit from high-density local storage without the need for external storage attachments.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong database and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded and database workloads.
tagline: A high-core compute-optimized platform featuring dense local NVMe storage and strong multi-threaded performance for database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 processors at 3.3 GHz.
3800 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong database operation benchmarks.
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for multi-threaded processing, software compilation, and database workloads.
